title: Clinical and biological significance of RAS mutations in multiple myeloma.

evidence_items.evidence_description: In a retrospective study of 439 patients with multiple myeloma enrolled in a clinical trial, 28 had mutations in KRAS (22/28 in G12/G13). Those with KRAS (but not NRAS) mutations had worse overall (median 19.9 vs 44.3 months, p=0.005) and progression free survival (median 14.7 vs 30.3 months, p<0.001) than those without mutations.
evidence_items.source_title: Clinical and biological significance of RAS mutations in multiple myeloma.

evidence_items.evidence_description: In patients with multiple myeloma, the presence of a mutation in NRAS was not significantly associated with progression-free or overall survival.
evidence_items.source_title: Clinical and biological significance of RAS mutations in multiple myeloma.

evidence_items.evidence_description: The presence of KRAS mutations in MM patients is prognostic for shorter overall and progression free survival.
evidence_items.source_title: Clinical and biological significance of RAS mutations in multiple myeloma.
